name: Choco (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the dense Pacific rainforest covering most of the territory
the gold, platinum and other mineral wealth of the department
the Pacific Ocean and the rivers that drain into both the Pacific and the Caribbean

description: Three horizontal stripes of green (top half), yellow (one quarter), and blue (bottom quarter).
history: Choco was created as an Intendancy in 1907 and elevated to a department in 1947. Its flag's three colours summarise the department's defining features: rainforest, mineral wealth, and the maritime border on the Pacific.

features.symbolism: green for the immense rainforest of Choco, yellow for the gold and platinum that have long been mined there, and blue for the two oceans (Pacific and Atlantic-bound rivers) that border the department
